definition: The structure of Organization > Folders > Projects > Resources in Google Cloud, used to organize resources and apply policies and IAM at the appropriate level.
context: IAM and org policies set at a higher level are inherited by everything below it in the hierarchy.

definition: A service that lets administrators set restrictions (constraints) on how resources can be configured across the resource hierarchy, such as restricting resource locations or disabling external IPs.
context: Policies set at the organization or folder level are inherited unless explicitly overridden where allowed.

definition: Google's identity-as-a-service product that lets organizations manage users, groups, and devices without requiring Google Workspace.
context: Creating a Cloud Identity account for your domain is what automatically provisions a Google Cloud organization node.

definition: A tool that synchronizes users, groups, and other data from an existing LDAP or Active Directory to Cloud Identity / Google Workspace.
context: Used for automated user provisioning rather than manually creating each Cloud Identity account.

definition: A service that provides a historical and current inventory of Google Cloud resources and IAM policies across an organization, exportable for analysis.
context: Useful for security audits, compliance checks, and answering 'what resources exist where' across many projects.

definition: An AI-powered assistant integrated into the Google Cloud Console that explains resource configurations, answers questions in natural language, and suggests optimizations.
context: Requires the Cloud AI Companion API to be enabled; provides recommendations but does not autonomously apply changes.

definition: A feature that allows external human identities (from providers like Okta, Azure AD, or any SAML/OIDC IdP) to access Google Cloud resources without creating Cloud Identity accounts.
context: Used for SSO-style access by employees using an existing corporate identity provider.

definition: A Google Cloud Billing feature that lets you set a spend threshold for a billing account or project and configure alert notifications at percentage thresholds.
context: Different from billing export — budgets are for alerting, export is for detailed cost data analysis.

definition: A feature that exports detailed Google Cloud billing and cost data to a BigQuery dataset for custom SQL-based cost analysis.
context: The standard way to perform deep, custom cost analysis beyond what the Billing Console dashboards provide.

definition: Limits Google Cloud places on resource usage (API calls, VM counts, etc.) per project to prevent abuse and manage capacity; increases can be requested via the Console.
context: A 429 RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED error typically signals a quota limit has been hit.

definition: Google Cloud's umbrella term for the suite of monitoring, logging, and tracing products (Cloud Monitoring, Cloud Logging, Cloud Trace, Cloud Profiler) that provide visibility into system behavior.
context: Full observability requires combining metrics, logs, and traces — no single product covers all three signals.

definition: An org policy constraint, also called Domain Restricted Sharing, that restricts which domains can be added as IAM principals across the organization.
context: Commonly used to block personal Gmail accounts from being granted access to corporate Google Cloud projects.

definition: An org policy constraint that restricts which physical locations (regions/multi-regions) resources can be created in.
context: Used for data residency and compliance requirements.

definition: Audit logs that capture all administrative actions that modify resource configuration or metadata, including IAM policy changes (SetIamPolicy). Enabled by default and cannot be disabled.
context: The authoritative source for tracking who changed what configuration and when.

definition: Audit logs that capture API calls that read or write user-provided data, such as reading objects from Cloud Storage. Disabled by default due to volume and cost.
context: Must be explicitly enabled per service if you need to track data reads/writes for compliance.
